# VER-QUIC-CRT-0017 -

## Scope

This verification covers the server-role 1-RTT packet-protection publication slice on the existing managed TLS seam.
It proves explicit directional open/protect material publication after the supported Finished proof boundary and keeps 1-RTT data-path support, key update, endpoint-host wiring, and interop-runner handshake support out of scope.

## Requirements Verified

- REQ-QUIC-CRT-0103
- REQ-QUIC-CRT-0117
- REQ-QUIC-CRT-0119

## Verification Method

Execution, inspection, and benchmark evidence.

## Preconditions

- The `Incursa.Quic` solution builds successfully.
- The CRT requirement traits are discoverable by the quality inventory.
- The benchmark suite and test suite remain runnable in the local workbench flow.

## Procedure or Approach

- Run the requirement-home tests that prove the server-role Finished proof boundary and the new 1-RTT publication slice.
- Run the bridge-state rejection tests for premature, repeated, conflicting, and fatal publication attempts.
- Run the client-role regression test that keeps the existing generic OneRtt packet-protection path intact while rejecting the server-only directional updates.
- Run the benchmark that exercises the server Finished publication path if the benchmark artifact is present.
- Re-run the JSON validation and markdown render checks so the trace surface stays synchronized.

## Expected Result

- The server role publishes explicit 1-RTT open/protect packet-protection material only after the supported `PeerFinishedVerified` boundary.
- Premature, repeated, conflicting, malformed, fatal, or terminal publication is rejected deterministically.
- Client-role generic 1-RTT packet-protection behavior remains intact while the new server-only directional updates stay rejected on client role.
- The verification lane does not imply 1-RTT data-path support, key update, endpoint-host wiring, or interop-runner handshake support.

- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTlsTransportBridgeDriver.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicConnectionRuntime.cs
